summary: DHH recommends the second edition of Programming Ruby by Dave Thomas, calling it his bible for Ruby syntax, style, and usage. He credits the book as the first resource he recommends to newcomers. He urges readers to buy the book on release day to repay Dave Thomas and Andy Hunt's generosity in releasing the first edition for free online. He frames the purchase as a way to prove Ruby is a growing language deserving more books, and teases an upcoming book of his own.
key_points: Programming Ruby served as DHH's primary reference from his earliest days with Ruby through the present
Dave Thomas and Andy Hunt released the first edition online for free, which DHH sees as an act of generosity worth repaying
Buying the book on release day would signal to publishers that Ruby is a viable, growing market
DHH hints at his own forthcoming book, likely Agile Web Development with Rails
money_quotes: From my initial dabbling with the language and to this very day, Programming Ruby has served as my bible on syntax, style, and usage.
Buy this book on the release day of October 3rd. And prove to publishers and authors that Ruby is a growing language that deserves more books.
It's time to repay their generosity.
Who knows, perhaps it'll encourage the writing of another book. You know of which I speak.
key_insight: Supporting Ruby book sales is both a way to repay the community's generosity and a strategic move to grow the Ruby ecosystem.
